title: Enterprise Responsive Design
description: Responsive design patterns for enterprise applications

principles.title: Mobile-First Not Mobile-Only
principles.description: Enterprise apps are primarily desktop but must work on tablet/mobile
approach: Design for desktop first (most common use case)
Ensure core tasks work on tablet
Mobile is for viewing/reviewing, not heavy data entry

principles.title: Content Priority
principles.description: What to show/hide at different breakpoints

patterns.desktop: Full navigation, all columns, side panels
patterns.tablet: Collapsible sidebar, reduced columns, slide-out panels
patterns.mobile: Hamburger nav, single column, bottom sheets

principles.title: Touch vs Click
guidance: Increase touch targets to 44x44px minimum on mobile
Add hover states for desktop, remove for touch
Consider swipe gestures for common actions on tablet
Bottom sheet modals on mobile vs center modals on desktop

patterns.name: Responsive Table
patterns.description: How tables adapt to smaller screens

approaches.name: Column Priority
approaches.implementation: Show essential columns, 'View more' expands full table

approaches.name: Horizontal Scroll
approaches.implementation: Freeze first column, horizontal scroll for rest

approaches.name: Card View
approaches.description: Transform rows to cards on mobile
approaches.implementation: Each row becomes a card with key-value pairs

approaches.name: Pagination Reduction
approaches.description: Fewer items per page on mobile

approaches.name: Bottom Navigation
approaches.description: Primary actions at bottom on mobile

approaches.name: Crumbs + Actions
approaches.description: Breadcrumbs at top, key actions floating

approaches.name: Stack Fields
approaches.description: All fields stack in single column

approaches.name: Full-Width Inputs
approaches.description: Inputs take full width on mobile

approaches.name: Floating Labels
approaches.description: Labels inside inputs to save space

approaches.name: Simplify
approaches.description: Show essential data points on mobile
approaches.implementation: Reduce data density, show trends not details

approaches.name: Tap for Details
approaches.description: Show data on tap instead of hover

testing: Test on actual devices when possible
Use browser DevTools responsive mode for quick checks
Test common enterprise devices (iPad, Surface, common Android tablets)
Consider touch vs mouse interaction differences
